mcqs.question: Who proposed the concept of Ethereum?
mcqs.explanation: Vitalik Buterin proposed Ethereum in his whitepaper in late 2013. Gavin Wood later co-founded the project and wrote the Yellow Paper (technical specification), but the original concept came from Buterin.

mcqs.question: What makes Ethereum fundamentally different from Bitcoin?

options.text: Ethereum uses a different hashing algorithm
Ethereum supports Turing-complete smart contracts and serves as a programmable blockchain
Ethereum has a faster block time
Ethereum uses a different data structure for blocks

mcqs.explanation: The fundamental difference is that Ethereum supports Turing-complete smart contracts, making it a general-purpose programmable blockchain platform, while Bitcoin's scripting is limited and not Turing-complete.

mcqs.question: Which transaction model does Ethereum use?
mcqs.explanation: Ethereum uses an account-based model where each account has a balance and nonce. This differs from Bitcoin's UTXO model where balances are calculated from unspent transaction outputs.

mcqs.question: What is the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)?

options.text: A physical server that runs the Ethereum network
A runtime environment for executing smart contracts on every Ethereum node
A virtual machine for mining Ethereum blocks
A cloud service provided by the Ethereum Foundation

mcqs.explanation: The EVM is a deterministic, sandboxed runtime environment that executes smart contract bytecode on every Ethereum node, ensuring all nodes compute the same results for the same input.

mcqs.question: Which type of Ethereum account can initiate transactions?

options.text: Both EOA and Contract accounts equally
Neither; transactions are created by miners

mcqs.explanation: Only Externally Owned Accounts (EOA), controlled by private keys held by users, can initiate transactions. Contract accounts can only execute code when triggered by an EOA or another contract.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of gas in Ethereum?

options.text: To measure the amount of ETH in a wallet
To measure and pay for computational work, preventing infinite loops and abuse
To speed up transaction confirmation time
To encrypt transaction data on the blockchain

mcqs.explanation: Gas is a unit of computational work on Ethereum. Every EVM operation has a gas cost. Users pay gas fees (in ETH) to compensate validators. Gas prevents infinite loops because contracts run out of gas and halt.

mcqs.question: What major event in 2022 changed Ethereum's consensus mechanism?

options.text: The Merge, transitioning from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake

mcqs.explanation: The Merge (September 2022) transitioned Ethereum from energy-intensive Proof of Work to Proof of Stake consensus, reducing energy consumption by approximately 99.95% while maintaining network security.

mcqs.question: What is 1 Gwei equal to in terms of Wei?
mcqs.explanation: 1 Gwei = 10^9 Wei (1,000,000,000 Wei). Wei is the smallest denomination of ETH: 1 ETH = 10^18 Wei = 10^9 Gwei.
